sourceMovie.title: Network
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Network works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Nightcrawler
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Network clicked for you, Nightcrawler (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Network clicked for you, Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Network?
faq.answer: Try Nightcrawler, Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri, Natural Born Killers—each shares DNA with Network while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Network?
faq.answer: Network (1976) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Network worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
